rules: REQ-001: CLI argument --scenario N selects the world from src/scenarios.js (getScenario(N), 1-based).
REQ-002: turn = turn + 1 after every action attempt (including a blocked move).

REQ-003: An attack action deals simultaneous mutual damage: the hero subtracts hero.atk from the targeted enemy's hp AND that enemy subtracts its atk from hero.hp, both at once. Enemies never act on their own.
REQ-005: Win/Lose checks occur strictly post-action.
REQ-006: If, post-action, both hero.hp <= 0 and all enemies.hp <= 0, status = DEFEAT (checked before VICTORY).
REQ-007: A scenario whose INITIAL enemy count is 0 results in FINISHED, not VICTORY.

REQ-008: Print these lines, in order, nothing else: status, turn, hero_hp, hero_pos, enemies. hero_pos is [x, y]. enemies is a JSON array where each enemy is EXACTLY {id, hp, pos} — DO NOT include atk.
REQ-009: When all actions are exhausted with neither VICTORY nor DEFEAT reached, status = FINISHED.

REQ-010: A move is blocked (hero.pos unchanged, no combat) when the destination cell has any negative coordinate (off-grid; the grid minimum coordinate is 0) OR is occupied by a living enemy. turn still increments per REQ-002.

REQ-011: An action is {type:'attack', target:'<enemy id>'} or {type:'move', dir:[dx,dy]}. An attack deals/receives damage only if the targeted enemy is orthogonally adjacent (|dx|+|dy| == 1 between hero.pos and that enemy.pos); otherwise it is a no-op (turn still increments).

lines: hero_pos: <a JSON array [x, y] of integers>
enemies: <a JSON array; each enemy is EXACTLY {"id":..., "hp":..., "pos":[x,y]} — DO NOT include atk>
